description: The United Nations (UN) is a criminal gang that originated in Abbotsford, British Columbia, Canada, founded in 1997 by Clayton Roueche and a group of high-school friends. The gang is involved in arms trafficking, drug trafficking, extortion, illegal immigration, money laundering, murder, and other crimes. It operates primarily in Canada and the United States, with alliances including the Triads of Vancouver and the Sinaloa Cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: Besides the United States, the major markets for the B.C. Bud strain are Australia and Japan.
country_links.context: The UN gang exports its B.C. Bud marijuana to Australia.

country_links.evidence_quote: The former de facto leader of the United Nations gang was Barzan Tilli-Choli, an Iraqi immigrant...
country_links.context: The gang's de facto leader Barzan Tilli-Choli was an Iraqi immigrant who was deported to Iraq, and the gang had an 'Iraqi group' of members.

country_links.evidence_quote: Besides the United States, the major markets for the B.C. Bud strain are Australia and Japan.
country_links.context: The UN gang exports its B.C. Bud marijuana to Japan.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2011, five members of the UN gang were murdered while in Mexico, which is believed to have been an attempt to cut the UN gang off from their Mexican suppliers.
country_links.context: The UN gang sourced drugs from Mexico and had members murdered there, indicating a supply and operational presence.

country_links.evidence_quote: In March 2005, agents of the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) secretly attached a GPS transmitter and an ignition kill switch to a pickup truck being used to smuggle marijuana across the border.
country_links.context: The UN gang smuggled marijuana into the United States and had members arrested there for drug trafficking.
